claims.evidence_text: The unknown factor is length of time needed at these low temps for vernalization; the range has been reported to be 30 to 75 days.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat vernalization requires roughly 30 to 75 days at low temperatures.

claims.evidence_text: Wheat seed germination occurs with soil temps in the upper 30s and vernalization occurs with temps at or below those same temps.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat vernalization occurs at soil temps at or below the upper 30s F; winter wheat will not produce seed if not vernalized.
